bio.quote: Bryn Ketilsdottir is a young Ember Keeper whose confidence was shattered when her sacred flame went dark during the winter surge. She is sharp-eyed, earnest, and carrying more guilt than she can name,

system.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

novice.description:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Resistance: 0

system.description: As a free action, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 meters, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.

novice.description: As a free action, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 meters, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.

system.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

novice.description: Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.

system.description: If frightened, slowed, or weakened, use a free action to end one condition.

novice.description: If frightened, slowed, or weakened, use a free action to end one condition.

system.description: Triggered action to take half damage from non-untyped damage (fire, cold, etc.).

novice.description: Triggered action to take half damage from non-untyped damage (fire, cold, etc.).

system.description:
Nomad lines that winter beside rib-cairns and bone-fields, keeping the old fires and the old taboos while carrying authority by rite and remembrance.

Language: Beinmarksmål

Environment: Nomadic; Organization: Communal; Upbringing: Noble

novice.description:

Language: Beinmarksmål

system.description:
Skills: Vigilant, Vigilant, Strong.

Languages: No additional languages.

Renown: 0; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 100

Perk: *Never Lost (On Roads)*. You can’t be made lost while traveling a route you’ve previously completed safely.

novice.description:
Skills: Vigilant, Vigilant, Strong.

Languages: No additional languages.

Renown: 0; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 100

Perk: *Never Lost (On Roads)*. You can’t be made lost while traveling a route you’ve previously completed safely.

system.description: Whenever a willing ally falls and would land on you or adjacent to you, you can safely catch them as a free triggered action. Neither of you takes damage from the ally’s fall.

novice.description: Whenever a willing ally falls and would land on you or adjacent to you, you can safely catch them as a free triggered action. Neither of you takes damage from the ally’s fall.

system.description: The power of the gods flows through you! As a vessel for divine power, you don’t just keep your allies in the fight. You make those allies more effective, even as you rain divine energy down upon your foes. Though the deity or saint you serve might have other faithful and clergy, you are special among worshippers, receiving your abilities from the highest source. As a conduit, you heal and buff your allies, and debuff your foes while smiting them with divine magic. The spark of divinity within you shines, filling your enemies with awe and making you more worldly and aware.

novice.description: The power of the gods flows through you! As a vessel for divine power, you don’t just keep your allies in the fight. You make those allies more effective, even as you rain divine energy down upon your foes. As a conduit, you heal and buff your allies, and debuff your foes while smiting them with divine magic. The spark of divinity within you shines, filling your enemies with awe and making you more worldly and aware.

system.description:
Piety: You gain 2 piety the first time in an encounter that a creature within 10 meters who isn’t a minion is reduced to 0 Toughness, or the first time in an encounter that a solo creature within 10 meters becomes winded.

Prayer Effect: You inflict a deadly curse on up to two enemies within 10 meters of you. Each target takes {corruption damage} equal to twice your Intuition score.

novice.description:
Piety: You gain 2 piety the first time in an encounter that a creature within 10 meters who isn’t a minion is reduced to 0 Toughness, or the first time in an encounter that a solo creature within 10 meters becomes winded.

Prayer Effect: You inflict a deadly curse on up to two enemies within 10 meters of you. Each target takes {corruption damage} equal to twice your Intuition score.

system.description:
Piety: You {gain 2 piety} the first time in an encounter that an enemy within 10 meters takes fire or holy damage.

Prayer Effect: One enemy within 10 meters takes {fire damage} equal to three times your Intuition score.

novice.description:
Piety: You {gain 2 piety} the first time in an encounter that an enemy within 10 meters takes fire or holy damage.

Prayer Effect: One enemy within 10 meters takes {fire damage} equal to three times your Intuition score.

system.description:
Your deity grants you a Heroic Resource called piety, letting you heal and empower your allies, and unleash holy power upon your foes.

Piety in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain piety equal to [[lookup @hero.milestones]]{your Milestones}. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 1d3 piety.

Additionally, you can gain more piety by praying to the gods—but beware! Doing so can easily draw their ire, as the gods hate to be annoyed. Before you roll to gain piety at the start of your turn, you can pray (no action required). If you do, your roll gains the following additional effects:

  • If the roll is a 1, you gain 1 additional piety but anger the gods! You take psychic damage equal to {1d6 + your level}, which can’t be reduced in any way.

  • If the roll is a 2, you {gain 1 additional piety}.

  • If the roll is a 3, you {gain 2 additional piety} and can activate a domain effect of your choice (see below).

You lose any remaining piety at the end of the encounter.

Piety Outside of Combat

Though you can’t gain piety outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost piety without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs piety, you can’t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Milestones or finish a extended rest.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited piety on its effect, such as Healing Grace, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of piety equal to your Milestones.

Domain Piety and Effects

Your choice of domains provides you with two additional ways to earn piety during combat, as triggered by specific events. You might even have a single event trigger both your piety effects. For example, the Sun domain grants piety when a nearby creature takes fire or holy damage, while the War domain grants piety when a nearby creature takes damage of 10 + your level or higher. If you have both those domains and a nearby creature takes an appropriate amount of fire damage, you gain piety from both your domains.

Additionally, whenever you activate a domain effect by praying for piety, you can choose one of your domains and have that domain’s prayer effect take effect immediately.

system.description: Your deity rewards you with hazy visions of things to come. Each time you earn 1 or more Milestones, you earn an equal number of fate points. Whenever you or a creature within 10 meters makes a test, you can spend 1 fate point to tap into a vision of the outcome, granting that creature an advantage (second chance) on the test. You lose any remaining fate points when you finish a extended rest.

novice.description: Your deity rewards you with hazy visions of things to come. Each time you earn 1 or more Milestones, you earn an equal number of fate points. Whenever you or a creature within 10 meters makes a test, you can spend 1 fate point to tap into a vision of the outcome, granting that creature an advantage (second chance) on the test. You lose any remaining fate points when you finish a extended rest.

system.description: Your god infuses wrath within your being. You gain a +1 bonus to rolled damage with magic abilities.

novice.description: Your god infuses wrath within your being. You gain a +1 bonus to rolled damage with magic abilities.

system.description: Invisible spirits surround you if you are harmed. Whenever an adjacent creature deals damage to you, they take {corruption damage} equal to your Intuition score.

novice.description: Invisible spirits surround you if you are harmed. Whenever an adjacent creature deals damage to you, they take {corruption damage} equal to your Intuition score.
